channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Ooni Of Ife Denies Conferring Chieftaincy Title On Baba Ijesha
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Ogunwusi, has denied reports circulating on social media alleging that he conferred a chieftaincy title on popular Nollywood actor and comedian, James Olanrewaju, popularly known as Baba Ijesha. This is contained in a statement issued on Saturday by the Director of Media

item.title: Faithfully Continuing With Christ Until The Crowning Day
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: LUKE 22:28–30-28 He wanted the highest Personality in heaven and earth to worship him. This is what the children of the devil do, they want everyone, high and low, preachers and hearers to bow and worship them. They keep taunting preachers of righteousness so that they would do their bidding. vs 10.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Families are expected to have respect for human life. According to a community member, Yahaya Kana Ismaila, the victim was attacked and killed while returning from his farm on Satu

item.title: Isa Hamma Dashen: The judge who can do anything and everything
item.dc:creator: ICIR Nigeria
item.description: By Chidi Anselm Odinkalu COURTS are creatures of law. Everything about them, including what they can do (jurisdiction) and how they can do it (procedure), is prescribed and regulated by law. As such, it should never be said that what a court or judge cannot do does not exist. In Nigeria, however, so

item.title: Falling Quality, Rising Prices: A Growing Consumer Crisis
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: What would you do if you bought a roll of “quality” toilet paper only to watch it flake apart as you unrolled it? item.title: FG Flags Off Rehabilitation Of Key Borno Roads
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Government has flagged off the rehabilitation of the Bama-Banki Road and Dikwa-Gamboru-Ngala Road in Borno State, describing the projects as strategic corridors for trade, agriculture, security and post-insurgency recovery in the North-East. The projects were flagged off on Saturday by V

item.title: Ex-Ogun first lady Olufunke Daniel loses mother
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former Ogun State First Lady, Olufunke Daniel, mourns the passing of her mother, Chief Sabainah Ibironke Opawole, who died peacefully at 93.
Read More: https://punchng.com/ex-ogun-first-lady-olufunke-daniel-loses-mother/

item.title: Navy uncovers hidden illegal diesel storage site in Rivers, recovers 11,800 litres
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigerian Navy has uncovered a concealed illegal fuel storage site in Rivers State, recovering approximately 11,800 litres of suspected illegally refined Automotive Gas Oil (AGO), commonly known as diesel, as part of its ongoing Operation Delta Sentinel. The post Navy uncovers hidden illegal dies

item.title: Abducted Oyo pupils: Rescue operation ongoing – Army Chief
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: He expressed confidence that the operation would lead to the safe rescue of the victims and their reunion with their families. The post Abducted Oyo pupils: Rescue operation ongoing – Army Chief appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Guéhi Labels Mexico Favourites Ahead Of England Clash
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Marc Guéhi has said he believes Mexico are the favourites for England’s World Cup last-16 clash at the Estadio Azteca. The reward for Wednesday’s 2-1 comeback win against the Democratic Republic of Congo is Sunday’s mouthwatering last-16 tie in Mexico City. A hostile atmosphere and

item.description: Media personality Toke Makinwa has shared strong opinions on dating and finances, urging men to choose partners within their wage bracket to avoid pressure and unrealistic expectations. item.title: England enter the Azteca - where football kings are crowned
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: BBC Sport explores the history and cultural power of the Azteca, one of the great cathedrals of world football, as England prepare to face Mexico there in the last 16 of the World Cup.

item.description: Global trade depends heavily on efficient container ports. Efficiency has become one of the most important measures of success in the global maritime industry. The post Full List: Top 20 most improved container ports in the World appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Nigeria mulls drastic diplomatic actions after two citizens killed in South Africa
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Federal Government has condemned the killing of two Nigerian nationals in South Africa, warning that it could consider further diplomatic measures, including unspecified options beyond dialogue, if attacks against Nigerians in the country persist. The post Nigeria mulls drastic diplomatic action

item.title: I don’t date single guys, I go for married men — Esther Nwachukwu
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ayo Onikoyi Controversial Nollywood actress Esther Nwachukwu has stirred fresh debate after revealing that she prefers dating married men over single men, describing them as more mature and understanding in relationships. The actress made the remarks during a recent appearance on The Honest Bunch

item.title: Sam Larry hospitalised as bouncer dies in Lagos road crash
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nigerian music promoter and socialite, Samsom Balogun, popularly known as Sam Larry, has been hospitalised after he was involved in a road crash on the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Road in Lagos State, while his bouncer reportedly died in the accident. The crash was said to have happened in the early hours
